findings.message: `Follow-up` claims that old ADR D4 (including `spec_source edge`) is fully superseded by the new ADR, but the new ADR still leaves `spec_source edge` as an open question (`Q`) and explicitly says its adoption is undecided. That makes the supersession map internally inconsistent: one part of D4 is still unresolved, so it cannot be described as wholly rewritten yet.

Decision C says entry shapes are fixed to subgraph rectangles because entries are subgraphized, but Decision F explicitly exempts `FunctionEntry` from subgraphization and places it as a standalone callable node. The shape rule therefore does not apply uniformly to all entries, so the renderer model is inconsistent for functions.

The FunctionEntry clause claims standalone function nodes can inherit 'Decision F's shape choice', but Decision F only defines Type/Trait entry subgraph behavior and method/variant nodes. As written, the styling rule for FunctionEntry is unsupported and leaves the function-node shape semantics ambiguous.

`ADR 2026-05-08-0248 D5` is the wrong source for the alleged 'syn-free principle'. In that ADR, D5 is about identifier/newtype wrappers, not parser placement or syn usage, so the rejection rationale points at an unrelated decision.

P1: [`knowledge/adr/2026-05-13-0000-contract-map-renderer-catalogue-v3-adaptation.md:246-249`](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/knowledge/adr/2026-05-13-0000-contract-map-renderer-catalogue-v3-adaptation.md#L246-L249) treats `TypeAlias` as a symmetric relation and renders it with an undirected edge. `TypeAlias.target` is directional in the schema, so this loses which symbol is the alias and which is the canonical target, which is semantically misleading for the rendered contract map.

The render-order section is not exhaustive: it enumerates classDefs, layer/module/entry subgraphs, method/variant nodes, crate-root entries, edges, and class attachments, but it never assigns a placement/order for `FunctionEntry` nodes. That conflicts with Decision C, F, and I, which require standalone function nodes to be styled and rendered by default, so the implementation still lacks a deterministic rendering rule for functions.

`Decision C` makes `FunctionEntry` styling depend on `[node.Function]`, but `Decision L` only defines node buckets as `Method`, `Variant`, and `Field`. As written, the config schema never actually allocates a `Function` node section, so the function-node styling path is left undefined in the document itself.

The render-order summary says the first block contains classDef definitions derived from `[class.*] + [edge.*]`, but the `[edge.*]` section is defined as arrow/label styling, not classDef data. That misclassifies edge configuration as class styling and makes the output-structure description semantically wrong.
